sections.paragraph:
Ἁλφαῖος, ου, ὁ [Heb.] also Ἀ., an Israelite name: Alphaius

—1. father of tax collector Levi Mk 2:14; Lk 5:27 v.l.

—2. father of James, one of the twelve especially chosen by Jesus; through the qualification ‘son of A.’ this James is distinguished from James the son of Zebedee: Mt 10:3; Mk 3:18; Lk 6:15; Ac 1:13.
